title: Integrate a real CC0 pixel-art pack behind the texture layer
description: Replace (or augment) the programmatic textures in src/game/textures.ts with a genuine CC0/public-domain pixel-art pack (e.g. Kenney) for terrain/biomes, structures and animals. Keep all art access behind the existing texture-key indirection so the scene is untouched. Add an ATTRIBUTION/licence note to the README. The hominin era-morph may stay hand-authored if no CC0 equivalent exists — say so.
verify: Game still builds and runs; biome/structure/animal sprites are sourced from a CC0 pack with attribution in the README; src/game/MainScene.ts changes are minimal (texture keys only); sim untouched.

title: Refine the 9-stage hominin morph (shading, silhouettes, animation hint)
description: Improve the per-era human sprite in src/game/textures.ts: cleaner silhouettes, better shading/dithering, a subtle idle bob or 2-frame walk, and clearer era-to-era clothing/tool reads. Keep textures keyed by morph signature so they stay deduped.
verify: npm run build passes; sprite textures still generated per morph key; no change to src/sim; a screenshot-able visible improvement described in AGENT_REPORT.

title: Deepen map exploration: fog-of-war + per-region discovery
description: Add lightweight exploration: regions start undiscovered and are revealed by migrating to adjacent ones (or scouting). Track discovered regions in sim state (serialize-safe) and reflect it in the map UI (src/ui/map.ts). Keep the sim pure; the map view only reads state.
verify: New unit test covers discovery state + a save/load round-trip including it; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; map UI shows undiscovered vs discovered regions.

title: Family tree: add a descendants view + search by id
description: Today the family tree is ancestry-focused. Add a toggle to view a focal individual's DESCENDANTS, and a small search box to jump to an individual by id. Drive entirely off existing lineage data (motherId/fatherId). No sim changes required.
verify: npm test/build pass; family-tree view offers ancestry/descendants toggle and id search; no src/sim changes; no console errors.

title: Add a soft food-storage cap so surplus can't grow unbounded
description: Mid/late game the food pool can balloon into the thousands, removing tension. Add a storage cap that scales with shelter tier / capacity (a tunable in the BALANCE block), so hoarding is bounded. Keep the autopilot able to reach the final era.
verify: New/updated test asserts food is bounded by the cap; npm test/build pass; npm run sim still reaches the Information Age within the sane year range; change confined to src/sim with a BALANCE entry.

title: Add a few choice-driven event chains
description: Add 2-3 small branching events (beyond the current one-shot hazards/bounties) that offer the player a choice with trade-offs (e.g. a hard winter: ration food vs. risk a hunt). Model them in the pure sim with a pending-choice mechanism mirroring the existing encounter API; the UI surfaces the choice.
verify: Unit tests cover each event's branches deterministically; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; encounter-style pending-choice pattern reused (no sim/render leak).

title: Add light era-aware ambient audio (WebAudio) and SFX, off by default
description: Extend src/ui/audio.ts with a gentle generative ambient pad that shifts subtly by era, plus a couple of SFX (birth, discovery). Must remain OFF until the player enables sound, and must never block on autoplay. Pure UI concern.
verify: npm build passes; audio stays off by default and only starts after a user gesture; no changes to src/sim; toggling sound works.

title: Cache the per-tick `living` list to cut O(n) filtering
description: Simulation.living filters the full individuals array (which grows with every birth) many times per tick. Cache the living list once per tick and invalidate on births/deaths/migration, without changing behavior. Determinism must be preserved exactly.
verify: All existing tests pass byte-identically (determinism intact: same seed → same winning year); npm build passes; a micro-benchmark or note in AGENT_REPORT shows fewer full-array scans; change confined to src/sim/simulation.ts.

title: Polish onboarding + first-session guidance
description: Improve the tutorial/first-run experience in src/ui/overlay.ts: clearer first steps, a hint pointing at the Map and Family buttons, and a dismissible 'next objective' nudge tied to state.goal. Keep it skippable and remembered in localStorage.
verify: npm build passes; tutorial still shows once and is dismissible/remembered; no sim changes; no console errors.

title: Accessibility pass: keyboard controls + contrast + ARIA
description: Add keyboard shortcuts (space=pause, 1/2/4=speed, m=map, f=family), ensure focus styles on interactive controls, check colour contrast on key text, and add ARIA labels to icon-only buttons. UI-only.
verify: npm build passes; documented keyboard shortcuts work; icon buttons have accessible labels; no sim changes.

title: Pool tribe sprites instead of create/destroy churn
description: MainScene currently destroys/creates hominin sprites as individuals come and go. Introduce a small object pool to reuse Phaser images, reducing GC churn at large populations. Render-only; behavior unchanged.
verify: npm build passes; game still renders the capped sprite set correctly; no src/sim changes; AGENT_REPORT notes the pooling approach.

title: Victory/extinction summary screen with charts
description: On win or extinction, show a richer summary: the era timeline (years per era), final vs starting trait averages, peak population, births/deaths/interbreds, and the longest-lived lineage. Render from sim state + the existing history the UI already samples. Pure UI; the sim exposes data, the overlay draws it.
verify: npm test/build pass; a summary appears on win and on extinction with at least the era timeline and trait deltas; no src/sim behavior change; no console errors.

title: Add a small achievements system
description: Track ~8 achievements (e.g. reach each era, interbreed with all three lineages, survive a run staying in the tundra, hit a population milestone, win without migrating). Compute them from sim state, persist unlocked ones to localStorage, and surface a small badges panel. Keep detection in a pure helper; UI just displays.
verify: Unit tests cover the achievement-detection helper deterministically; npm test/build pass; unlocked achievements persist across reloads; no sim/render-split violation.

title: Difficulty presets (gentle / standard / harsh)
description: Add selectable difficulty presets that scale a few existing BALANCE/config knobs (event lethality, abundance, starting food/pop). Wire a selector on the new-run flow; thread the choice through GameController into SimConfig. The sim stays pure (it just receives config); presets live in one small data table.
verify: New test asserts each preset produces a valid, completable config (autopilot still reaches the Information Age on standard); npm test/build pass; preset selectable in the UI; sim untouched except reading config.

title: Biome-aware weather/ambience particles
description: Extend MainScene's existing weather overlay with light, biome-appropriate ambience: dust in the desert, drifting leaves in the forest, sea-spray/birds on the coast, heat-shimmer tint, etc. Cheap, capped particle counts; render-only; respects the day/night + cold overlays already present.
verify: npm build passes; particles vary by biome and stay performant (capped counts); no src/sim changes; framebuffer still renders.

title: Name and surface notable individuals
description: Give individuals procedural names and let the Chronicle/family tree highlight 'notable' ones (longest-lived, most descendants, highest in a trait, first of a lineage). Names + notability are derived from existing lineage/genome data in a pure helper; the UI displays them.
verify: Unit tests cover the naming + notability helpers; npm test/build pass; names appear in the family tree/inspector; no sim/render-split violation.

title: Visual tech-tree graph (prereq edges)
description: Upgrade the tech panel (or add a modal) that draws the tech tree as a graph with prerequisite edges and era columns, showing known/available/locked state and research progress. Reads TECH_TREE; pan/zoom optional. Pure UI on top of existing knowledge data.
verify: npm test/build pass; the graph shows prereq edges and per-tech state correctly; clicking an available tech still sets the research target; no src/sim changes.

title: Keep late-game smooth at large populations
description: Profile the per-tick cost as population and the retained-individuals array grow into the thousands over a long run, and cut the worst offenders (allocations in hot loops, repeated full scans) without changing behaviour. Determinism must be exactly preserved.
verify: All tests pass byte-identically (same seed -> same winning year); npm build passes; AGENT_REPORT shows a measured reduction in per-tick work or allocations; change confined to hot paths.

title: Quest/objective system (pure sim)
description: Add a pure-sim quest model: a handful of objective-driven quests (e.g. 'reach 30 population', 'discover fire before year 30', 'interbreed with all three lineages', 'survive a winter event chain', 'found a settlement in a new biome') with live progress tracking, completion, and a small reward (food/materials/a one-off bonus). Quests live in state, are serialize-safe, and are evaluated each tick. No rendering.
verify: New unit tests cover quest progress + completion + reward deterministically and a save/load round-trip including quest state; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; src/sim only, no Phaser/DOM.

title: Quest log panel
description: Surface active and completed quests in the overlay: title, one-line description, a progress bar, and the reward. Reads quest state from the sim; updates as progress changes; a subtle toast/log entry on completion.
verify: npm test/build pass; quests render with live progress and a completion cue; no src/sim changes; no console errors.

title: Expand the choice-driven event chains (6+ new)
description: Build on the existing EVENT_CHAINS system: add at least six new branching events with real flavor and sim-affecting outcomes (e.g. a prophet/seer, a great migration omen, a feud, a bountiful flood, a stranger bearing knowledge, a sacred-site discovery). Each option has a clear trade-off; keep the cautious=0 / risky=1 convention.
verify: Unit tests cover each new event's branches deterministically; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; pending-choice pattern reused (no sim/render leak).

title: Lore codex / encyclopedia
description: A data-driven codex of short lore entries for techs, biomes, lineages, and events, unlocked as the player discovers each. A new 'Codex' panel lists unlocked entries with their flavor text. Lore content is data (a table) the UI reads; discovery state can derive from existing knowledge/sim state.
verify: npm test/build pass; entries unlock as their subject is discovered and read correctly; lore data is well-formed (a small test can assert every tech/biome has an entry); no sim/render-split violation.

title: Readable dynastic chronicle (history book)
description: Generate a readable 'history of the tribe' from the run: named notable individuals (uses the naming + notable-individuals work), era milestones, disasters/bounties, interbreedings, and quest completions — woven into short prose entries by year. A Chronicle/History view displays it. Pure UI over existing log + lineage data.
verify: npm test/build pass; the history view reads coherently from a real run; helper that composes entries is pure + unit-tested; no src/sim changes.

title: Flavor dialogue on encounters & milestones
description: Add short, data-driven flavor/dialogue lines that appear on encounters, event chains, era changes, and notable births/deaths — giving the tribe a voice. Lines are a content table keyed by situation; the UI picks one (seeded for determinism if shown in sim-driven contexts).
verify: npm test/build pass; lines appear in the right situations and never block; content table is well-formed; no sim/render-split violation.

title: Rival/neighbour tribes (pure-sim model)
description: Introduce one or more lightweight AI neighbouring tribes that exist on the region map, each with its own population, strength, era/tech level, home region, and a disposition toward you that drifts over time. They grow/decline on their own simple rules. Pure sim, serialize-safe, deterministic. No diplomacy actions yet — just their presence and evolution.
verify: New unit tests cover rival creation, per-tick evolution, and a save/load round-trip; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; src/sim only.

title: Diplomacy: relations + periodic encounters with rivals
description: Add a relations score per rival and periodic diplomacy events (a gift, a border tension, a request) that shift it, surfaced via the existing pending-choice mechanism so the player can respond. Outcomes adjust relations and resources. Pure sim + the existing choice API.
verify: Unit tests cover relation shifts + each diplomacy outcome deterministically; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; no sim/render leak.

title: Conflict & raids with hostile rivals
description: When relations sour, a rival can raid you (and you can respond). Outcome scales with each side's strength, population, defensive tech (hunting/bronze/iron/gunpowder), and shelter tier; losses fall on both sides per the existing mortality model. Keep it bounded and deterministic.
verify: Unit tests cover skirmish outcomes deterministically (stronger/better-defended side fares better); npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; balance not broken; src/sim only.

title: Trade with friendly rivals
description: With a friendly rival, allow trading food/materials/knowledge for mutual benefit (e.g. trade surplus food for a tech-research boost or a resource you lack). A periodic trade offer via the choice mechanism. Pure sim + choice API.
verify: Unit tests cover the trade exchange deterministically; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; no sim/render leak.

title: Neighbours on the map + diplomacy panel
description: Show rival tribes on the region map with their disposition (colour/icon), and a small diplomacy panel listing each neighbour with relations and any available action (gift/trade/threaten) wired to the sim's diplomacy API. UI reads sim state only.
verify: npm test/build pass; rivals render on the map with correct disposition and the panel actions call the sim; no src/sim changes; no console errors.

title: Carryable resources (wood / stone / hide)
description: Add a small set of gathered resources beyond food (e.g. wood, stone, hide) with per-biome availability, gathered by assigned workers, that gate building shelters and some tech/era progress. Fold the tunables into the BALANCE block. Pure sim; keep the run completable.
verify: Unit tests cover resource production + that shelter/tech gating respects them; npm test/build pass; the autopilot still reaches the Information Age in range; src/sim only.

title: Surface the new resources in the UI
description: Show wood/stone/hide stocks and their production in the resources panel, and reflect gating (e.g. a shelter upgrade waiting on stone). UI reads sim state.
verify: npm test/build pass; resource stocks + gating display correctly; no src/sim changes; no console errors.

title: Scouting: reveal regions & find resources/events
description: Let the player assign scouts (from the labour pool) to explore: over time they reveal adjacent fog-of-war regions and can surface a resource cache or a small event. Builds on the existing fog-of-war discovery. Pure sim + the existing task/choice systems.
verify: Unit tests cover scouting revealing regions + its outcomes deterministically; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; src/sim only.

title: Found a second settlement (sim model)
description: Allow founding a second camp in a discovered region, splitting off some population; each settlement tracks its own shelter, production, and local biome pressures, while sharing knowledge/culture. Start with exactly two settlements and a clean serialize-safe model. Ambitious but bounded — focus on a correct, tested sim model (UI can come later).
verify: New unit tests cover founding, per-settlement production, and a save/load round-trip; npm test/build pass; the autopilot still reaches the Information Age; determinism preserved; src/sim only.

title: Make seasons matter (scarcity & growth windows)
description: Deepen the existing season cycle so winter meaningfully tightens food/production and spring/summer boost it, nudging migration and storage decisions — without making the game unfair. Tune via BALANCE. Pure sim.
verify: Updated tests assert the seasonal swing and that the autopilot still reaches the Information Age in range; npm test/build pass; change confined to src/sim BALANCE/world logic.

title: Leaders / chieftains with tribe-wide effects
description: Each generation has a leader (a notable living individual) whose standout traits grant small tribe-wide bonuses (e.g. a strong leader boosts defense, a smart one boosts research, a speechful one boosts cooperation). On a leader's death, a succession event picks the next. Pure sim + tests; surfaces via existing chronicle/notable data.
verify: Unit tests cover leader selection, the trait-driven bonus, and succession deterministically; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; src/sim only.

title: Culture / belief progression
description: Add a culture/belief track (parallel to the language chain) that accrues from rituals/burial/art and unlocks small cohesion bonuses and belief-flavored events. Data-driven effects aggregated like tech. Pure sim + tests.
verify: Unit tests cover culture accrual + its effects; npm test/build pass; sim still reaches the Information Age; src/sim only; effects are data-driven.

title: Tribe policies with trade-offs
description: Let the player pick one or two standing policies with genuine trade-offs (e.g. communal vs competitive: cooperation/research vs individual fitness pressure; expansion vs consolidation: capacity vs stability). Threaded through config/state into the existing fitness/production math. Keep the game completable under any choice.
verify: Tests assert each policy's effect and that the autopilot still wins under the default; npm test/build pass; the UI exposes the choice; sim reads policy state only.

title: Epidemics that scale with density & era
description: Make disease richer: occasional epidemics whose severity scales with population density, biome, and era, mitigated by medicine/sanitation/vaccines, and which select harder on diseaseResistance. Bounded and deterministic; must not make the game unwinnable.
verify: Unit tests cover epidemic severity + mitigation deterministically; npm test/build pass; the autopilot still reaches the Information Age; balance preserved; src/sim only.

title: Selectable start scenarios
description: Add a few distinct start scenarios (e.g. Frozen World, Lush Valley, Lonely Island, Crowded Lands) that set starting region/biome, population, resources, and pressures for varied challenge. Builds on the difficulty-presets work; scenarios are a config table; the sim just receives config.
verify: A test asserts each scenario yields a valid, completable config (autopilot reaches the Information Age on the standard ones); npm test/build pass; scenario is selectable in the new-run flow; sim untouched except reading config.

title: Optional run mutators
description: Add toggleable run modifiers at new-run (e.g. harsh disease, abundant game, fast eras, ice age, hostile neighbours) that adjust existing config/BALANCE knobs for replay variety. Thread through config; combine cleanly.
verify: Tests assert mutators apply and the game stays completable in sane combinations; npm test/build pass; mutators selectable in the UI; sim reads config only.

title: Generative era-aware background music
description: Layer a gentle, generative musical bed on the existing WebAudio ambient (off by default) that shifts mode/instrumentation by era — Paleolithic drones to a fuller Information-Age texture. No heavy assets; synthesized. Pure UI; never autoplay.
verify: npm build passes; music stays off until enabled and only starts after a user gesture; shifts by era; no src/sim changes.

title: Richer hominin animation
description: Add more life to the tribe sprites: a couple more walk/idle/work frames and small era-appropriate motion, building on the existing 2-frame walk, still keyed by morph signature so textures stay deduped. Render-only.
verify: npm build passes; sprites animate more richly without per-frame texture explosion; no src/sim changes; framebuffer still renders.

title: Juicy feedback for key events
description: Add tasteful visual/audio feedback for births, deaths, discoveries, raids/battles, and quest completions — floating text, small particle bursts, and the existing SFX hooks — so the world feels alive. Render/UI only; performant and capped.
verify: npm build passes; feedback fires on the right events and stays performant; no src/sim changes; no console errors.

title: Overlay polish: transitions, tooltips, iconography
description: A cohesive polish pass on the DOM overlay: smooth panel transitions, helpful tooltips on stats/controls, clearer icons, and tightened layout/spacing — keeping the warm pixel aesthetic. UI/CSS only.
verify: npm build passes; the overlay looks cleaner and tooltips work; no src/sim changes; no console errors.

title: Split the growing simulation.ts into modules
description: As simulation.ts has grown, extract cohesive concerns (events, reproduction, production, world/season, rivals) into focused modules with no behaviour change. Pure refactor; the public Simulation API and determinism must be preserved exactly.
verify: ALL tests pass byte-identically (same seed -> same winning year); npm build passes; no behaviour change; the sim/render split is intact; only src/sim files moved/split.

title: Versioned saves that round-trip the new systems
description: Give the save format an explicit version and safe upgrade path so saves round-trip all the newer systems (quests, rivals, resources, settlements, culture) and older saves still load with sensible defaults. Pure sim/serialization.
verify: Tests cover a full round-trip of the new state and loading a prior-version save with defaults; npm test/build pass; determinism preserved on resume; src/sim only.
